description:
Blue Sky Plan 5 is open with a mandibular CBCT case containing a pre-placed dental implant at tooth #30 (lower right first molar). This implant plan requires quality assurance review before proceeding to surgery.

Verify the implant placement against these clinical safety standards:
- Minimum 2.0 mm clearance from the implant apex to the inferior alveolar nerve canal
- Implant body fully contained within bone — no perforation of buccal or lingual cortical plates at any level
- Implant reasonably centered within the ridge (buccal and lingual bone walls each ≥ 1.0 mm thick)

Measure the current nerve clearance, buccal bone wall thickness, and lingual bone wall thickness. If any criterion is violated, correct the implant placement to achieve full compliance with all criteria simultaneously.

Save the following to C:\Users\Docker\Documents\QAReview\:
- before_correction.png — cross-section at #30 before any changes, with measurement annotations
- after_correction.png — cross-section at #30 after corrections, with updated measurements
- qa_report.txt — original measurements (nerve clearance, buccal wall, lingual wall in mm), each violation identified, corrections applied, and final post-correction measurements
- reviewed_plan.bsp — the corrected project file
